analysis_result: 8 of 18 MPNST samples (44%) with NF1 mutations in locally reproduced WES analysis; full cohort analysis yields 24 of 54 WES samples (44.4%) with NF1 mutations
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA SOURCES
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 rows, 15 cols; filtered to WES='X' -> 54 samples (16 Neurofibroma, 20 Atypical_NF, 15 MPNST, 3 Low-Grade MPNST, 0 Cutaneous_NF)
   - MOESM5 (WES mutations): 7,457 rows covering 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcode values (2 of 54 WES samples had no detected mutations)
   - Paper text + Fig 2 captions (paper.pdf pages 4-5)

B. QUESTION INTERPRETATION DISCREPANCY
   The question asserts the Fig 2 oncoprint used 55 MPNSTs with NF1 at 55%. The paper text actually states:
   - Fig 2a caption: n=55 samples (TMB analysis, benign+atypical+MPNST combined)
   - Fig 2b caption: n=54 samples (WES oncoprint)
   - Main text: '23 of 55 samples (42%) harboring somatic NF1 mutations'
   - Main text: 'NF1 mutations were observed in 44% of MPNSTs, 60% of atypical neurofibromas and 18% of benign neurofibromas'
   The 55 refers to the full benign+atypical+MPNST WES cohort (not MPNST alone). The question formulation conflates '55 MPNSTs' with the full PNST WES cohort. The reported 23 of 55 (42%) is an overall frequency across all WES samples (paper's text), while oncoprint labels 55% for NF1 which disagrees with 42% in text.

C. COHORT DEFINITION (local reproduction)
   - Filter WES='X' from MOESM4 -> 54 samples total (matches paper Fig 2b n=54)
   - MPNST-only interpretation: Tumor Type in {MPNST, Low-Grade MPNST} -> 18 samples (matches paper's combined 18 MPNSTs mentioned for SUZ12/EED: '4 of 18' and '1 of 18')

D. COMPUTATION
   - NF1 mutations (any variant class) in MOESM5: 33 rows, 24 unique samples
   - Non-silent NF1 (Missense/Nonsense/Frameshift/Splice/InFrame): 33 rows, 24 unique samples (all NF1 muts are non-silent)
   - Samples with NF1 muts by tumor type: MPNST=6, Low-Grade MPNST=2, Atypical_NF=12, Neurofibroma=2, Cutaneous_NF=0 -> total 22 matched; 2 unmatched sample IDs ('1869', 'M2242', 'RT3379') were unmatched with clinical table
   - MPNST (combined, 18 samples): 6+2 = 8 with NF1 mutations -> 8/18 = 44.4% (matches paper's stated 44% of MPNSTs exactly)
   - All 54 WES: 24/54 = 44.4% with NF1 mutations (NOT 55% as oncoprint label suggests, NOT 42% as text states 23/55)
   - Atypical_NF: 12/20 = 60% NF1 (matches paper exactly)
   - Benign NF: 2/16 = 12.5% Neurofibroma + 0/0 Cutaneous NF = 12.5% overall (paper says 18%)

E. DISCREPANCY WITH REPORTED 23/55 (42%)
   - Paper reports n=55 but supplementary Data shows n=54 WES samples (1 sample count discrepancy)
   - Paper text (42%) differs from oncoprint label (55%) in same figure - internal paper inconsistency
   - Our analysis confirms 24 of 54 = 44.4% (close to 42% text but NOT 55% oncoprint label)
   - For 'MPNST' interpretation specifically: 8/18 = 44% matches paper's stated MPNST frequency exactly

F. RELATIVE DIFFERENCE
   - Reported: 42% (23/55); Model: 44% (8/18 MPNST) or 44.4% (24/54 all WES)
   - Percentage point difference: ~2.4 percentage points; relative difference ~5.7%
   - Both within 10% relative but interpretation of denominator differs significantly (55 vs 54 vs 18)

G. ROOT CAUSE
   The question formulation in questions_with_answers.xlsx conflates 'MPNST samples' with the full PNST WES cohort (n=54 in oncoprint, reported as 55 in text). The paper reports 23/55 (42%) for NF1 across ALL WES samples (benign+atypical+MPNST combined), NOT MPNST-only. When the model correctly filters to MPNST (the literal question), it gets 8/18 (44%), which exactly matches the paper's 'NF1 mutations were observed in 44% of MPNSTs' statement. The discrepancy is due to ambiguous question phrasing combined with the paper's own n=54 vs n=55 inconsistency between Fig 2a (TMB) and Fig 2b (oncoprint).
proposed_fix: Rephrase question: the paper's 23 of 55 (42%) refers to ALL WES samples (benign+atypical+MPNST combined), not MPNST alone. The MPNST-specific NF1 rate is 44% (8/18), which matches the paper's text statement 'NF1 mutations were observed in 44% of MPNSTs'. Also note paper's internal inconsistency: Fig 2a says n=55, Fig 2b oncoprint says n=54, and MOESM4 has 54 WES samples. The reported 55% NF1 on oncoprint conflicts with text statement 42% - likely oncoprint label shows highest-subgroup frequency.
